source_preflight.notes: 7 nodes (GEN, GATE, RUN_NOW, SILENT, READ, FOUR_Q, RUN_CONF). All bold labels <= 4 words. All map to named corpus phrases: 'AI generates code' (§4/§5.3), 'Read first?' (§6 Step 1), 'Run immediately' (§8 anti-pattern), 'Silent failure risk' (§5.2), 'Read whole block' (§6 Step 2), 'Four-question test' (§5.5), 'Run — confirmed' (§6 Step 5). No scope-creep. No unexplained acronyms.

stats.layout: flowchart LR (two direction:TB subgraphs — side-by-side panels)

color_assignment.SILENT: fail (#ffd6d6) — terminal risk: Silent failure risk

findings.detail: 7-node LR side-by-side contrast diagram. GEN (blue entry) feeds GATE (yellow decision diamond). GATE routes 'No' to PATH_A (purple RUN_NOW -> red SILENT) and 'Yes' to PATH_B (blue READ -> blue FOUR_Q -> green RUN_CONF). Two-path contrast intent fully realized. No overlaps, no truncation, no illegible edges expected at 1400px width.

All 7 nodes map to topic 11.8 corpus only. Subtitle text 'What / Where / Why / Spec?' accurately abbreviates the four questions from §5.5 without introducing external terminology. 'Silent failure risk' correctly names the §5.2 concept. 'Run — confirmed' accurately paraphrases §6 Step 5 ('running is a confirmation, not a discovery').

GATE uses :::hitl (yellow) which is the correct tier for a human judgment point — the decision to read before running is the professional discipline checkpoint. RUN_CONF uses :::done (green) and SILENT uses :::fail (custom red) giving clear visual contrast between the two path outcomes. No color tier violations.

verdict: PASS (cycle 1). 7-node LR side-by-side contrast. GEN (blue) -> GATE (yellow) -> PATH_A: Run immediately (purple) -> Silent failure risk (red) | PATH_B: Read whole block (blue) -> Four-question test (blue) -> Run confirmed (green). All labels <= 4 words, all corpus-grounded, no acronyms, correct color tiers, layout matches two-path contrast intent.
